verses.1: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind,
verses.4: “Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth?
verses.5: Who determined its measures, if you know?
verses.6: What were its foundations fastened on?
verses.7: when the morning stars sang together,
verses.8: “Or who shut up the sea with doors,
verses.9: when I made clouds its garment,
verses.10: marked out for it my bound,
verses.11: and said, ‘You may come here, but no further.
verses.12: “Have you commanded the morning in your days,
verses.13: that it might take hold of the ends of the earth,
verses.14: It is changed as clay under the seal,
verses.15: From the wicked, their light is withheld.
verses.16: “Have you entered into the springs of the sea?
verses.17: Have the gates of death been revealed to you?
verses.18: Have you comprehended the earth in its width?
verses.19: “What is the way to the dwelling of light?
verses.20: that you should take it to its bound,
verses.21: Surely you know, for you were born then,
verses.22: Have you entered the storehouses of the snow,
verses.23: which I have reserved against the time of trouble,
verses.24: By what way is the lightning distributed,
verses.25: Who has cut a channel for the flood water,
verses.26: to cause it to rain on a land where there is no man,
verses.27: to satisfy the waste and desolate ground,
verses.28: Does the rain have a father?
verses.29: Whose womb did the ice come out of?
verses.30: The waters become hard like stone,
verses.31: “Can you bind the cluster of the Pleiades,
verses.32: Can you lead the constellations out in their season?
verses.33: Do you know the laws of the heavens?
verses.34: “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds,
verses.35: Can you send out lightnings, that they may go?
verses.36: Who has put wisdom in the inward parts?
verses.37: Who can count the clouds by wisdom?
verses.38: when the dust runs into a mass,
verses.39: “Can you hunt the prey for the lioness,
verses.40: when they crouch in their dens,
verses.41: Who provides for the raven his prey,
